title: Intensely Flavorful Korean Shrimp Bowl
description: A fresh and juicy Korean dish featuring salted butter and boiled shrimp, served in a dolsot stone bowl, perfect for kids and pescatarians alike.
instructions: Heat the dolsot stone bowl in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
In a large pot, boil 4 cups of water and add the shrimp, cooking for 2-3 minutes or until pink and fully cooked.
In a small bowl, whisk together the gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, and garlic.
Add the cooked shrimp to the bowl and toss with the gochujang sauce until the shrimp are fully coated.
Add the salted butter to the shrimp and sauce, stirring until the butter is fully melted and the sauce is smooth.
Add the cooked white rice to the dolsot stone bowl, followed by the shrimp and sauce mixture.
Sprinkle the chopped green onions and sesame seeds over the top of the shrimp and rice.
Serve immediately, being careful not to touch the hot stone bowl.
